input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Your `BindingContext` is never set, so the bindings have nothing to resolve against. You need to assign your ViewModel to the page's `BindingContext`. Two common ways:

**1. In XAML:**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:YourApp.ViewModels"
             ...>
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

**2. In code-behind:**
```csharp
public MyPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    BindingContext = new MyViewModel();
}
```

Also ensure your ViewModel raises `PropertyChanged` notifications (e.g., implement `INotifyPropertyChanged` or inherit from `ObservableObject` in the MVVM Community Toolkit) so the UI updates when property values change.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The labels are blank despite the ViewModel having values. Looking at the XAML, I can identify the problem without needing any tools — this is a knowledge question.

The issue is that the `BindingContext` is never set. The XAML has `{Binding Title}` and `{Binding Description}`, but without setting the `BindingContext` to an instance of the ViewModel, the bindings have nothing to resolve against.

Additionally, the ViewModel needs to implement `INotifyPropertyChanged` (or use `ObservableObject` from MVVM Toolkit) for the UI to update when properties change.


I should provide a clear, actionable solution showing how to set the BindingContext and implement property change notifications.
